sample.title: Why South Korea’s AI Stock Mania Is a Warning to the World
sample.description:
South Korea’s stock market has surged about 200% year-on-year, powered by retail investors chasing an artificial intelligence-fueled chip rally. But with extreme volatility and a heavy reliance on two companies, is this a sustainable boom or a bubble waiting to burst? 

For more on what's happening in stocks, bonds, currencies and commodities and what will move them next, subscribe here to Bloomberg Markets Daily.
https://www.bloomberg.com/account/newsletters/markets-daily

sample.title: Why McDonald's is supersizing in China
sample.description:
China is a major source of McDonald’s growth and it plans to have 10,000 locations in mainland China by the end of 2028.

CNBC's Eunice Yoon explains how the chain is winning over Chinese consumers with both value and nostalgia.

Find the full report in related video.

sample.title: This New Medicare Pilot Program Offers GLP-1s For $50 A Month. Here’s What You Need To Know
sample.description:
Americans on Medicare can now purchase GLP-1s for just $50 a month thanks to a temporary pilot program called Bridge.  Patients who meet certain requirements, like having  BMI of over 35 or having obesity-related conditions, may not qualify. CNBC’s Annika Constantino has more.

Reporting by: Annika Constantino

sample.title: ICE Could Sign A $220M Contract With A Taser Maker That Trump Invested Millions In
sample.description:
In February 2026, President Trump invested up to $5 million in top U.S. taser-maker Axon Enterprise. Two weeks later, ICE announced that it was seeking a $220 million contract to more than quadruple its inventory. Experts say that Axon is the only company able to meet the requirement outlined by ICE. CNBC’s Luke Fountain breaks it down.

Reporter: Luke Fountain
Produced by: Macklin Fishman
Edited by: Andrea Miller
Animation: Jason Reginato, Emily Park
Shot by: Charlotte Morabito
Senior Managing Producer: Shawn Baldwin

sample.title: Inside The Rise And Fall Of Kohl's
sample.description:
Kohl's rose to its peak as a department store in the 2000s, with a focus on a strong in-store experience, coupons and rewards. Now, after years of stagnant sales and a rough patch on Wall Street, Kohl's is trying to get back to what made it a household name.

Reporter: Laya Neelakandan
Produced by: Tasia Jensen
Animation: Jason Reginato, Emily Park
Senior Managing Producer: Tala Hadavi

sample.title: PLL's Paul Rabil On The League's $100 Million Raise, Team Ownership And Olympic Opportunity
sample.description:
In this week's episode, CNBC's Alex Sherman sits down with Paul Rabil, Co-Founder and President of the Premier Lacrosse League.

They discusses how the PLL's recent $100 million fundraising round positions the league to eventually introduce team ownership, a key milestone in its long-term growth strategy. He also explains why lacrosse's return to the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics could help accelerate the sport's popularity, investment and commercial momentum.

Watch the full conversation above, and sign up to receive future editions of the CNBC Sport newsletter straight to your inbox: https://www.cnbc.com/lander?id=sport-newsletter

Reporter: Alex Sherman
Senior Field Producer: Jessica Golden
Senior Managing Producer: Tala Hadavi
Edited by: Tim Hurt
Animation: Jason Reginato
Additional Footage: Getty Images, PLL

For access to live and exclusive video from CNBC subscribe to CNBC PRO: https://cnb.cx/42d859g

sample.title: Agentic trading will give everyday investors institutional-level power: Robinhood CEO
sample.description:
AI agents will soon have the ability to match the capabilities of human traders, Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev predicts.

The power of agentic technology — AI that can carry out tasks for users — has been touted as potentially transformational by many in the tech sector, with industry giants including OpenAI and Anthropic racing to develop such products.

sample.title: Mark Zandi: Fed independence is critical for markets and the economy
sample.description: Mark Zandi, Chief Economist at Moody's Analytics, said underlying job growth remains soft, inflation should ease with wages, and the Fed is likely to keep interest rates unchanged for now.

sample.title: LIVE: Rescue teams try to pull out a survivor in Venezuela
sample.description:
International and Venezuelan rescue teams try to pull out an earthquake survivor from a collapsed mall. Footage released on July 1 showed guard Hernan Alberto Gil, trapped in a Venezuelan mall for over a week, talking to rescuers as they battled to free him from the rubble. Alberto Gil, 44, is trapped some 29 feet underground, according to the Costa Rican Red Cross.

#venezuela #earthquake #rescue #caracas #redcross #live #Reuters #News

Keep up with the latest news from around the world: https://www.reuters.com/

sample.title: Russian air strikes leave many dead in Kyiv
sample.description:
Russian forces attacked the Ukrainian capital Kyiv, killing more than a dozen people and wounding over 50, as drones and missiles struck residential buildings in what Russia called a "massive attack" on military and energy facilities, as well as airports in Kyiv and other locations.
 
#russia #ukraine #war #kyiv

sample.title: Eight monks killed in Thailand after 11-year-old boy crashes truck
sample.description:
Eight Buddhist monks were killed and more than 20 injured after being hit by a pickup truck driven by an 11-year-old boy while on a pilgrimage in Thailand's northeastern province of Mukdahan, officials said. This video contains graphic images.
 
#thailand #monk #truck #pilgrimage

Venezuela death toll rises and tens of thousands still missing after twin earthquakes | BBC News
sample.description:
A week on from the devastating earthquakes in Venezuela, the number of the dead has risen to nearly 2,300.
 
Tens of thousands of people are still unaccounted for, as the interim President Delcy Rodríguez has announced seven days of mourning.
 
Communities coming together with rescue teams from around the world are still frantically searching through the rubble.
 
The BBC’s International Correspondent Yogita Limaye was at one of the worst hit areas, La Guaira.
 
Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news

#Venezuela #BBCNews

sample.title: 1.8m people affected by Venezuela earthquakes, Unicef says. #Venezuela #BBCNews
